title: %Warning-REALCVT with variable of type time
body:
Attempting to verilate the following code on master (``verilator --binary --exe --build -j `nproc` timecvt.sv -o timecvt``):
```sv
`timescale 1ns/1ps

time T = 9ns;

module top(); endmodule
```

outputs a warning:
```
%Warning-REALCVT: timecvt.sv:3:10: Implicit conversion of real to integer
    3 | time T = 9ns;
      |          ^~~
                  ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/REALCVT?v=5.027
                  ... Use "/* verilator lint_off REALCVT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

Interestingly, doing one of the following things all cause the warning to go away:
- Removing the line `timescale 1ns/1ps`
- Changing the constant to e.g. `8ns`
- Removing the unit from the constant, so `time T = 9;`

I haven't looked into what the cause of this might be, but it sounds like some kind of floating-point rounding issue, possibly?
